response.justification: While the candidate has extensive experience in software development across multiple languages and domains, the resume lacks clear evidence of C++ expertise at scale, industrial automation exposure, or compliance with IEC 62443 security standards. The technical stack leans heavily toward JavaScript/TypeScript, Node.js, and D, with only minimal mention of C++ (e.g., National Instruments role). Leadership and mentorship are demonstrated, but the gap in core C++ and industrial cybersecurity competencies raises concerns for this role.
strengths: Strong leadership and mentorship experience with cross-functional teams
Demonstrated ability to architect and deliver scalable, event-driven systems (e.g., GISCollective, re:cinq)
Experience with secure development practices (TDD, CI/CD) and high-performance low-latency applications
concerns: Limited explicit C++ experience at scale (only brief mention in National Instruments role)
No clear evidence of IEC 62443 or industrial cybersecurity standards implementation
Lack of exposure to industrial automation protocols (e.g., Modbus TCP/IP, OPC UA) or cloud platforms (Azure)
